hypothesis.statement: On scheduled options-expiration (OPEX) and quad-witching sessions, we test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on the known-ahead OPEX-day flag and time-to-expiration differs from non-OPEX sessions (a 'pinning'-type conditioning hypothesis). Falsifiable; not a profitability claim.
hypothesis.economic_rationale: Dealer hedging of expiring options positions can concentrate flow near large open-interest strikes into the expiration; whether this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve. The strike/open-interest detail is NOT assumed here; the conditioner is the calendar position alone unless paid options data is approved.

new.name: is_opex_day_flag
new.reason: OPEX / quad-witching dates are DERIVABLE from the exchange calendar (third-Friday rules, quarterly cycle) and are known ahead; needs a new FeatureRequest extending SESSION_CALENDAR_ROLL. The existing minutes_to_expiration feature uses SessionCalendarRollMetadata.expiration_ts_by_contract_id (FUTURES contract expiration), which is related but NOT the options-expiration calendar; do not conflate them.

new.name: is_quad_witch_day_flag
new.reason: Quarterly quad-witching session flag; derivable from the exchange calendar; same family extension.

lookahead_risk.description: OPEX/quad-witching dates are fully deterministic from the exchange calendar and known years ahead, so the calendar conditioner itself carries little lookahead hazard. The hazard appears only if strike/open-interest 'pin' levels are introduced (those require point-in-time options data and could leak realized positioning).
lookahead_risk.mitigation: Restrict the conditioner to known-ahead calendar flags and time-to-RTH-close / time-to-expiration clocks. Any strike-level or open-interest input is out of scope here (needs paid options data) and would require its own card and point-in-time guard.

expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.statement: Conditioning on a deterministic options-expiration calendar is structurally distinct from the rejected realized-price/quote main effects. Because the hypothesized 'pinning' is a price-path outcome, the OUTCOME measurement may overlap with vwap_session/return_to_vwap dynamics; orthogonality of the OUTCOME must be checked, but the CONDITIONER is independent of any dead-mechanism input.
expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.diagnostic_check: Residualize forward outcomes against vwap_session and regime factors before scoring the OPEX-day conditioner; report marginal contribution. Cross-check that OPEX flag is not merely a proxy for day_of_week or month-end already captured elsewhere.

capacity_turnover_note: Approximately 12 monthly OPEX + 4 quad-witching sessions per year; low occurrence count, N_eff-limited but higher than the macro-print mechanisms. Pool across index futures per Track-B. No capacity/turnover dollar claim.

data_dependency.detail: OPEX (third-Friday) and quad-witching (quarterly) dates are deterministic calendar rules requiring no external feed. This is the lowest-data-dependency card. Strike-level options data (open interest, pin strikes) is paid data and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this card.

value_free_status.claims: hypothesis pre-registration only; no alpha/tradability/profitability claim
